target.name: Neuralink
target.founder: Elon Musk, Max Hodak, Benjamin Rapoport, Dongjin Seo, Paul Merolla, Philip Sabes, Tim Gardner, Tim Hanson, Vanessa Tolosa

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Groq
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $7B

data.narrative:
Neuralink, a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Elon Musk and others, is currently valued at $9.7B after raising $1.3B+. The peer universe consists of nine same-sector comparables at similar valuation tiers, including both private companies (Figure AI at $39.0B, Applied Intuition at $15.0B, Skild AI at $14.0B, Crusoe Energy at $10.0B, and Wayve at $8.6B) and one public reference point (Palantir). The peer set spans a broad range from $2.0B to $39.0B.

Peer-median analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. The median valuation across the nine same-sector peers is $8.6B, with a range spanning $2.0B to $39.0B. This method directly reflects Neuralink's positioning within the AI & Software private market at the scale tier it occupies.

The triangulated valuation range is $7.3B to $9.9B, centered on a median of $8.6B. The current $9.7B valuation sits at the upper end of this range, approximately 12% above the peer median. Key uncertainties include the limited applicability of single-method analysis and the wide dispersion in peer valuations, which reflects divergent risk profiles and stage dynamics within frontier AI. The current valuation appears defensible but assumes Neuralink commands a modest premium relative to median comparables.
